_comment: WC2026 Group G, Matchday 2 (SoFi Stadium, LA). MD1: Belgium 1-1 Egypt (poor); Iran 2-2 NZ. All on 1 point. Odds Belgium -230 (1.435) / Draw +360 (4.60) / Iran +650 (7.50). XI: Iran 5-4-1 (Taremi alone) = extreme low block; Belgium with De Bruyne + Lukaku. Calibrated IN LINE with the market (Spain lesson: don't go contrarian without strong evidence).

meta.stakes: Group G with all 4 on 1 point. Belgium favorite but came in poor (1-1 Egypt); a gutsy Iran (2-2 NZ) sets up a 5-4-1 to hold and counter through Taremi.

home.name: Belgium
notes: With De Bruyne (creator) + Lukaku (true striker) starting: HAS the tools to break down a low block (unlike Spain vs Cape Verde). attack 1.45.
Calibrated in line with the market (66-68%): Spain lesson, no going contrarian. My naive bottom-up was overvaluing Iran.
Defense: Courtois ok but the team conceded to Egypt -> defense 0.87.

away.name: Iran
notes: 5-4-1 with Taremi ALONE up top: minimal attack -> attack 0.55. That's why I see their win chance (10%) below the market (12.7%).
Organized back five but not impenetrable (leaked twice to NZ) -> defense 1.05.
Sits deep: cagey match with few goals -> rho -0.07.

cards._referee: Dario Herrera (Argentina), CONFIRMED. Strict/CONMEBOL referee: career ~4.9-5.7 YC/match. Discount for the international context but still high; slight bump because Iran sits deep with tactical fouls. Cards market relatively HIGH due to the referee factor.
